showTagline: Conversations with people whose stories changed mine.
hostShortBio: Master barber, recovery advocate, and long-form conversation host. 25 years behind the chair, reformed from addiction, drawn to the kind of talks that actually move people.
hostLongBio: Eric Falgout is a master barber based in Metairie, Louisiana with over 25 years behind the chair. A native of the Greater New Orleans area, Eric started the show after his own recovery from addiction — crediting long-form conversation, Joe Rogan's podcast guests, and the hard work of getting honest with himself as the things that saved his life. The show is built on the conviction that the most important stories don't come from celebrities. They come from paramedics, musicians, fighters, fishermen, comedians, and the person next to you at the counter. Eric records in the Greater New Orleans area and interviews guests whose experiences have shaped him or someone he knows.
showDescription: On this podcast I interview people from different walks of life whose life stories have made an impact on myself or someone else. Each episode is designed to take people on a journey through their most vulnerable truths — their emotions, thoughts, and behaviors — in a non-judgmental way.
showDescriptionLong: Every guest on this show has a story worth hearing. Paramedics who've seen the worst of humanity and still show up. Musicians chasing the sound that got away. Comedians building jokes out of the hardest years of their lives. People who've been to the bottom and back, and people who just kept going. The conversations are long, unrushed, and honest. If you like Rogan-style long-form but wish there was more of the real human underneath, you'll find a home here.
brandPositioning: Real conversations. Real people. Recorded in New Orleans.
